provenance.title: Volatility Modeling in Markovian and Rough Regimes: Signature Methods and Analytical Expansions

authors.name: Elisa Alòs
authors.affiliation: Universitat Pompeu Fabra and Barcelona School of Economics

authors.name: Òscar Burés
authors.affiliation: Universitat de Barcelona; IESE Business School

authors.name: Josep Vives
authors.affiliation: Universitat de Barcelona; Institut de Matemàtiques, Universitat de Barcelona

provenance.abstract_truncated: We study two complementary methodologies for calibrating implied volatility surfaces: analytical approximations and data-driven models based on rough path theory. On the analytical side, we revisit a second-order asymptotic expansion for the Heston model, and propose a new, VIX-based calibration scheme for the rough Bergomi model. In parallel, we develop a signature-based approach in which volatility is represented as a linear functional of the truncated signature.
key_claims: Analytical second-order expansion (ASV) calibrates Heston model parameters with errors ~1e-4 in implied volatility.
A new VIX-based closed-form calibration procedure for the rough Bergomi model is introduced, estimating H, η, ρ, σ₀ sequentially from short-maturity data.
Volatility can be represented as a linear functional of the truncated signature of a primary stochastic process (signature-based model).
In the Heston setting, signature-based calibration matches analytic accuracy (~1e-4 IV errors).
In the rough Bergomi setting (non-Markovian), signatures slightly outperform analytical methods, capturing complex temporal dependencies.

architecture.description: Two parallel calibration frameworks: (A) Analytical expansions for Heston and rough Bergomi, (B) Signature-based stochastic volatility model. Both produce implied volatility surfaces over a grid of strikes/maturities.

components.name: HestonAnalyticalExpansion
components.role: Calibrates Heston parameters (σ₀, ν, κ, θ, ρ) from ATM and skew term structures

components.name: RoughBergomiVIXCalibration
components.role: Sequentially estimates H, η, ρ, σ₀ from VIX ATMI and implied vol skew

components.name: TimeAugmentation
components.description: Augment primary path Xt → X̂t = (t, Xt) ∈ R ⊕ V

components.name: TruncatedSignatureComputer
components.description: Computes truncated signature S(X)^{≤N}_t for N=3. For a 2-dim path (time + primary), signature up to level N has sum_{k=0}^{N} 2^k components = 15 total at N=3.

components.name: SignatureVolatilityModel
components.description: σt(ℓ) = ℓᵀ · vec(S(X)^{≤N}_t), a linear combination of signature coordinates

components.name: QMatrixAssembler
components.description: Assembles 15×15 symmetric matrix Q(T) from shuffle products of signature entries at level 2N+1=7. Q(T)_{L(I),L(J)} = -½ <(eI⊔eJ)⊗e0, S(X)^{≤7}_T>

components.name: MonteCarloOptionPricer
components.description: Prices European calls by Monte Carlo using the signature-driven price process formula

components.name: CalibrationOptimizer
components.description: Minimizes weighted least-squares loss L(ℓ) between model and market option prices

data_flow: simulate primary process X via Euler scheme
compute S(X)^{≤7} for all paths (offline, once)
given ℓ: evaluate S̃_T(ℓ) per path → C(K,T,ℓ)

equations.name: Heston Stock SDE
equations.latex: dS_t = rS_t dt + \sigma_t S_t d(\rho W_t + \sqrt{1-\rho^2}B_t)

equations.name: Short-T Heston IV expansion
equations.role: calibration: smile at short maturities (x=log S, k=log K)

equations.name: Long-T Heston IV expansion
equations.role: calibration: ATM level at large T

equations.name: σ₀ regression from ATM term structure
Signature-based volatility
equations.role: core model — linear functional of truncated signature

equations.name: Q matrix definition
equations.role: assembles variance term via shuffle product and extended signature

equations.name: Calibration loss function
equations.weights: γ_i = inverse Vega of i-th option

training_pipeline.description: Two-phase: (1) offline signature computation, (2) online optimization of ℓ

steps: Simulate nMC paths of W, B (Gaussian increments, Euler scheme)
Simulate primary process X (Euler, dt determined by T_steps)
Compute truncated signature S(X)^{≤7} for all paths (GPU vectorized)
Compute stochastic integrals ∫vec(S^{≤3}_s)dZs via Euler sum
Assemble Q(T) matrices per maturity, compute Cholesky
phase1_offline.note: All computed once and cached before optimization

phase2_optimization.constraints: box constraints on ℓ components (paper does not specify exact bounds)
phase2_optimization.initial_guess: not specified (assumption: zeros or random small values)

hyperparameters.T_steps: not explicitly stated (assumption: ~100-200 Euler steps per unit time)

heston_primary_params_uncorrelated.note: X0 stated as 0.1 in Sec 5.1; but equation says dσ²=..., so X0=σ₀²=0.04 or X0=0.1 as variance — paper states X0=0.1 for the SDE (4.2)

evaluation_protocol.datasets: Synthetic market prices from Heston MC / rough Bergomi MC (ground truth)
metrics: Absolute implied volatility error per contract: |IV_model(Ki,Ti) - IV_mkt(Ki,Ti)|
Loss function value L(ℓ*) at convergence

compute.hardware: Consumer desktop, 128 GB RAM, NVIDIA RTX 3080 Ti GPU

baselines: Rough Bergomi VIX calibration (new, this paper)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Euler-Maruyama discretization with ~100-200 time steps per unit time for primary process
implementation_assumptions.basis: Common practice for SDE simulation; paper states Euler scheme but not dt

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Stochastic integral ∫vec(S^{≤N}_s)dZs computed via left-point Euler sum over time grid
implementation_assumptions.basis: Standard MC approximation for Itô integrals; consistent with Euler scheme for X

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Box constraints on ℓ set to [-10, 10] per component
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper mentions box constraints to accelerate convergence but does not specify bounds

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Initial guess for ℓ is zero vector
fBM simulation via Cholesky or Hybrid scheme (Bennedsen et al. 2017) for rough Bergomi market generation; Euler with exact fBM increments for primary process
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper cites Bennedsen et al. 2017 and McCrickerd & Pakkanen 2018 for market prices; primary process uses standard Volterra kernel simulation

implementation_assumptions.assumption: PyTorch or NumPy + CuPy used for GPU computation of signatures
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper references Peter Foster's code adapted for GPU; likely PyTorch or CuPy

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Signature computed via log-ODE or Chen's identity on linear interpolation of discrete path
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says linear interpolation is used (Section 4.3)

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Weights γ_i = 1/Vega_i computed under Black-Scholes with ATM vol = σ₀
implementation_assumptions.basis: Inverse Vega weighting is standard practice; paper states γ_i = inverse Vega

ambiguities.description: X0 for Heston primary SDE stated as 0.1, but equation (4.2) is variance (σ²). Ambiguous whether X0=0.1 means initial variance=0.1 (so σ₀≈0.316) or initial vol=0.1 (σ₀=0.1)
ambiguities.primary_assumption: X0=0.1 is the initial variance, since the SDE is for variance (matching market σ₀=0.2 → variance=0.04 doesn't match; paper explicitly states X0=0.1 so treat as given)

ambiguities.description: Number of Euler time steps not specified
Exact box constraint bounds for L-BFGS-B not stated
